title: Syntax error in Dockerfile doesn't generate an error, produces useless image
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

If a Dockerfile has a VOLUME call with a path that doesn't start with a slash, podman build does not return an error but produces a useless image.

Trying to run this image locks up podman. `podman ps` hangs.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

Build and run an image with the following dockerfile:

```dockerfile
FROM alpine:3

VOLUME oops

CMD [ "/bin/sh" ]
```
For example:

```
$ podman build -t example .
STEP 1: FROM alpine:3
STEP 2: VOLUME oops
--> a0a4f313979
STEP 3: CMD [ "/bin/sh" ]
STEP 4: COMMIT example
--> c30345b1c16
c30345b1c164a93d4c45097435d84669c5b038e158354194d232f6ed8b6b574a
$ podman run example
[... nothing happens, just hangs ...]
```

**Describe the results you received:**

Podman locks up for the user.

**Describe the results you expected:**

Preferred: podman build fails due to the syntax error
Alternate: the image doesn't lock up podman

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

Reproduced on two systems:

```
Version:      3.1.2
API Version:  3.1.2
Go Version:   go1.16.3
Built:        Wed May 12 12:27:59 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

```
Version:      3.0.2-dev
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.16.1
Built:        Thu May 20 09:22:46 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-3.1.2-3.fc34.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

Not tested with other versions, did review the troubleshooting guide.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Both machines are physical, amd64.
